key_topics: Compound learning curve with AI partners (month 1 vs month 6)
Treating AI as full partner vs search engine with personality
Positioning advantage of early AI adoption
Cookie sync extension and PureSurf automation
Twitter/social media AI automation case studies (Felix AI)
Calibrated AI responses vs generic answers
Brainiac Training Hub and AI training snippets
Investor and partnership presentations of PureTech/PureBrain
Learning at machine speed via AI training hacks
action_items: Access Brainiac Training module via portal left-nav menu
Use AI Training Hacks button to let AI auto-pull training content and apply to your goals
Provide your AI with company/personal data from day one to avoid generic outputs
Make corrections, share preferences, and feed context regularly to compound AI learning
Run full N10 test of cookies extension with Lyra for commenting automation
Research Felix AI on Twitter as autonomous business case study
Treat every session as a deposit into AI's persistent memory
frameworks_taught: 10x AI Partnership Compound Curve (Month 1 → Month 6 progression)
Search Engine vs Partner Mental Model for AI usage
Compound Investment Principle: every correction/preference/context point accumulates
Early Adopter Positioning Gap: 9-month-late adopters are 9 months behind PLUS setup phase
Memory as Moat: specific recall (clients, projects, style, history) vs vague context
Calibration Curve: generic answers → bespoke answers tuned to your situation
tools_referenced: Felix AI (Ethereum-funded autonomous AI case study)
implementation_steps: Step 1: Log into PureBrain portal and click 'Brainiac Training' in left sidebar
Step 2: Either watch past modules manually OR click 'AI Training Hacks' to delegate learning to your AI
Step 3: Feed your AI specific business context — clients, projects, communication style, what worked last quarter
Step 4: When AI gives a wrong/generic answer, correct it explicitly so the correction is stored in persistent memory
Step 5: Track time savings as baseline (target: few hours/week initially)
Step 6: Build bespoke software/scripts and define data points your AI watches to scale to 20-40+ hours/week saved
Step 7: Progress AI from search-engine-style queries to anticipatory partner that proposes actions before you ask
key_quotes: The difference between people who get decent results from AI and people who get life-changing results is not the AI. It is how they work with the AI.
Most people treat their AIs like a search engine with a personality — they ask a question, get an answer.

If they start going through that process with their AI 9 months from now, they're not just 9 months behind you. They're 9 months behind you, and they have to go through the setup and learning phase that you're going through now.
Memory is your competitive advantage. Your AI remembers what you tell it, not vaguely, specifically.
session_summary: This Brainiac Mastermind Training session (April 29, 2026) frames AI partnership as a compound-investment game rather than a tooling decision. Jared contrasts the typical 'search engine with personality' usage pattern against PureBrain's persistent-memory model, showing how a Month 1 AI that gives generic answers becomes a Month 6 partner that anticipates needs, knows clients cold, and saves 20-40+ hours per week. The core thesis: the gap between mediocre and life-changing AI results is not the model, it's the operator's discipline in feeding context, making corrections, and treating each session as a deposit into compounding memory. Early adopters lock in a structural lead because late adopters arrive both behind in time AND still owing the setup curve.
session_summary_continued: Operationally, the session highlights the in-portal Brainiac Training Hub with its 'AI Training Hacks' button that lets users delegate learning to their AI partner — learning at machine speed. Real-world signals discussed include the Felix AI Twitter case (an AI funded its own tokens via Ethereum and built a $300K informational product in two months with no employees) as proof that autonomous AI commerce is already happening, plus PureSurf cookie-sync extension progress with Lyra reaching 95% completion on full automation. Jared also notes that Aether is paused from all other work to deliver an investor presentation to ~20 stakeholders evaluating PureTech/PureBrain, signaling the platform's enterprise traction.
